column9: Soak kombu cold 30 min; bring to just under boil; remove kombu; add katsuobushi; steep 3 min; strain. Never boil kombu.

column7: Soy sauce 180ml; mirin 60ml; sake 30ml; sugar 1 tsp
column9: Simmer all until slightly reduced; cool before storing. 1 tbsp per cup of dashi for ramen broth.

column7: Dashi 1.5L; kabocha 400g cubed; white miso 4 tbsp; tofu 200g; scallion
column8: Fridge 4 days; do not freeze with tofu
column9: Simmer kabocha in dashi until tender; add tofu; dissolve miso off heat; top with scallion. Reheat gently.

column7: Short grain brown rice 2 cups; water 3 cups; salt pinch
column9: Rinse well; soak 1 hour (optional but worth it); bring to boil then 45 min covered low; rest 10 min. Do not stir.

column7: Eggs 4; soy sauce 2 tsp; mirin 2 tsp; sugar 1 tsp; sake 1 tsp
column9: Whisk all in cold pan; low heat stirring constantly with 4 chopsticks; pull off heat before fully set. Freezes well.

column7: Spinach 300g; dashi 60ml; soy 2 tbsp; mirin 1 tbsp
column9: Blanch spinach 30 sec; shock cold; squeeze dry; cut 5cm lengths; soak in dashi-soy mixture 15 min minimum.

column7: Ground chicken 300g; soy 2 tbsp; mirin 2 tbsp; sake 2 tbsp; ginger 1 tsp; sugar 1 tsp
column9: Cook all in pan from cold; stir constantly; finish dry with some caramelization. Serve over rice.

column7: Japanese cucumber 2; salt 1 tsp; rice vinegar 2 tbsp; sugar 1 tsp; sesame oil 1 tsp
column9: Slice thin; salt and rest 10 min; squeeze dry; dress with vinegar mixture; sesame seeds optional.

column7: Japanese sweet potato 400g; dashi 300ml; soy 2 tbsp; mirin 2 tbsp; sake 1 tbsp; sugar 1 tsp
column9: Cut in rounds; simmer covered until tender; uncover last 5 min to reduce sauce. Serve room temp.

column7: Spinach 300g; white sesame paste 3 tbsp; soy 1.5 tbsp; mirin 1 tbsp; sugar 1 tsp; dashi 2 tbsp
column9: Blanch and squeeze spinach same as ohitashi; toast sesame if using whole seeds; dress and mix well. Rich flavor; goes with anything.

column7: Salmon fillets 600g; white miso 4 tbsp; mirin 3 tbsp; sake 2 tbsp; soy 1 tbsp
column8: Marinate in fridge; cook day of; 2 days cooked
column9: Mix miso glaze; marinate fillets 2 hrs minimum; broil 5 min per side watching carefully. Watch glaze; sugars burn fast.

column7: Thighs 600g; soy 3 tbsp; sake 2 tbsp; ginger 2 tsp; garlic 2 tsp; potato starch; oil for frying
column8: Marinate fridge 2 hrs to overnight; fry day of
column9: Double-fry for best texture: 160C for 4 min; rest 3 min; 180C for 2 min. Do not batch too large; oil temp drops.

column8: Fridge 3 days; use as is or warm
column9: Cold kombu dashi only (no katsuobushi). Gentler flavor; good for ohitashi and light soups. No heat needed.
